rulings.Case Title: FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. RALPH TASHJIAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff National Title Insurance Companys Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 22, 2019 line 10. Plaintiff National Title Insurance Companys Motion For Summary Adjudication DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden and show that it is entitled as a matter of law to a judgment on a cause of action for breach of implied covenant as a subrogee. See Points and Authorities 4-8. The Court further notes that moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b); no "specific cause of action, affirmative defense, claims for damages, or issues of duty" are stated in the separate statement. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JUANA Q. RODRIGUEZ VS. DORA E. RODRIGUEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement Under Code Of Civil Procedure 664.6
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 22, 2019 line 1. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement Under Code Of Civil Procedure 664.6 Hearing Required to address specific relief requested under the current circumstances and the lapsed reverse mortgage application. No substantive opposition filed. Procedural/jurisdictional objections raised in the opposition are overruled. Plaintiff's motion is not a CCP 1008 motion; the Court has jurisdiction per CCP 664.6; March 13, 2017 filing; Mesa RHF Partners v. City of Los Angeles 2019 WL 1416925. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES AUSTIN ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Alternatively For Issue Sanctions, Evidentiary Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 22, 2019 line 2. PLAINTIFF JAMES AUSTIN, RAYMOND SCHREIBER Notice Of Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Alternatively For Issue Sanctions, Evidentiary Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants DENIED in part and GRANTED in part. Request for terminating sanctions and issue sanctions is denied. Julia Mwangi shall appear for deposition on a mutually agreeable date within 15 days of this order on a 5 business day notice. If parties cannot agree on a date, deposition shall take place on May 2, 2019, Plaintiff to provide notice of time and location. Plaintiff may conduct further discovery re: up to 3 newly identified witnesses only. Sanctions granted in the amount of $3,960. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: IGOR GAVRILOV ET AL VS. JAMIE CHANG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 22, 2019 line 3. DEFENDANT JAMIE CHANG, HIEN NGUYEN MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED as to Causes of Action 1-7. Defendant's Undisputed Material Facts #5-6 states "[a]t the time Defendants served Plaintiffs with the second rent increase notice, Defendants anticipated in good faith and under serious consideration that if Plaintiffs failed to comply with the notice, Defendants would serve Plaintiffs with a Three Day Notice to Pay Rent or Quit. If Plaintiffs failed to cure the Three Day Notice to Pay Rent or Quit, Defendants would have filed an action against Plaintiffs for unlawful detainer." (emphasis added.) Defendants provided sufficient admissible evidence to shift the burden to Plaintiffs, and Plaintiffs did not dispute these facts for the purposes of the motion. Under Bisno v. Douglas Emmett Realty Fund 1988 (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 1534,1553, where "litigation was contemplated in good faith and under serious consideration at the time the notice was sent" the litigation privilege is triggered. Accordingly, Defendants are entitled to a Motion for Summary Adjudication as to all causes of action resulting from the rent increases. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: RS LENDING, INC. VS. ECHO PARK COOPERATIONS LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Expunge [Second] Notice Of Pendency Of Action From Record
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 22, 2019 line 4. PLAINTIFF RS LENDING, INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Expunge [Second] Notice Of Pendency Of Action From Record GRANTED for the reasons outlined in the moving papers. No opposition filed. Attorney's fees and costs granted in the amount of $8,258. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MARC ROVETTI VS. KIPLING C NIELSEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Cmpel Deposition And Production Of Documents; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration O Fkipling Nielsen In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 22, 2019 line 7. DEFENDANT DOLORES NIELSEN, KIPLING NIELSEN Notice Of Motion And Motion To Cmpel Deposition And Production Of Documents GRANTED, subject to opposition. By no later than April 25, 2019 Plaintiff to appear and sit for a deposition; produce documents per the Notice of deposition. Parties to meet and confer regarding a mutually agreeable date. If parties cannot agree on a date, deposition shall take place on April 25, 2019. Trial is continued to May 6, 2019 for Plaintiff to comply with its discovery obligations. Hearing required to address the amount of sanctions requested. Defendants to provide the specifics regarding $1,000 request, e.g. costs, fees incurred to prepare the motion, etc. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: BRIDGE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT COMPANY VS. SUAMELIE PESE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 22, 2019 line 8. DEFENDANT BEAUTAINA PESE DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT OFF CALENDAR, Third Amended Complaint on file. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ERIC W. WONG VS. ROSA CORADO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 22, 2019 line 9. DEFENDANT ROSA CORADO, OSCAR HERRERA, ANGELA SHOCKNESS, YESSENIA HERRERA, JOSE SARABIA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Under the circumstances of this case and the nature of the title held as community property, the Notice should have been executed by both spouses. This requirement reconciles the purpose of the Rent Ordinance and FC 1102 and puts the burden on the correct party. = (501/CFH)
